findings.title: Privacy Risk: Data Handling Gaps
findings.description: Detected privacy risk keywords indicating potential gaps in PII handling and consent mechanisms. The current data handling practices may not align with the data-minimal principle and retention policy.
assumptions: Current data handling practices are not fully compliant with privacy policies.

evidence.excerpt: Detected privacy risk keywords in the documentation.

recommendations: Review and enhance data handling practices to ensure compliance with data minimization and retention policies.

findings.title: Critical Security Risk: Authentication Weaknesses
findings.description: Identified significant weaknesses in the authentication mechanisms, which could lead to unauthorized access and data breaches.
assumptions: Current authentication methods may not meet industry standards.

evidence.excerpt: Detected auth risk keywords in the documentation.

recommendations: Implement stronger authentication mechanisms, including multi-factor authentication.

findings.title: High Compliance Risk: Data Isolation
findings.description: The current reliance on application-level row filtering for tenant data isolation poses a compliance risk, particularly for SOC 2 Type II requirements.
assumptions: Application-level row filtering is insufficient for compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Current reliance on application-level row filtering for tenant data isolation poses a compliance risk.

recommendations: Implement multi-tenant data isolation methods to meet compliance requirements.

findings.title: High Compliance Risk: Data Residency Controls
findings.description: Enterprise customers require data residency controls to comply with local laws, which are currently lacking in the platform.
assumptions: Data residency controls are necessary for compliance in regulated markets.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise customers require data residency controls to comply with local laws.

recommendations: Enable data residency controls to allow enterprise customers to choose where their data is stored.

findings.title: High Security Risk: Role-Based Access Control Gaps
findings.description: The current role-based access control (RBAC) system is too simplistic, leading to security concerns and inefficiencies.
assumptions: Current RBAC system does not meet enterprise security needs.

evidence.excerpt: Current RBAC model is too simplistic, leading to inefficiencies and security concerns.

recommendations: Develop a fine-grained RBAC system that allows for custom role definitions and resource-level access controls.

summary: Identified 5 risks (2 compliance, 1 privacy, 2 security). 3 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (4 blockers, 1 warnings).
